what_it_does: Git ignore rules for the company repo: excludes virtualenvs, Python caches, node_modules, build/runtime store data, secrets and TLS credentials, Obsidian per-user workspace/cache state, CLI telemetry, and the-one-system regenerable scratch directories.
observations: Runtime store data ignored: store-data/, .data/ ('written by the live system, never code'), .build/.

Secrets ignored with inline NEVER-commit comments: .secrets, .boundary.env/*.boundary.env, and Tailscale Funnel TLS cert+key by both glob (*.ts.net.key/.crt) and exact filename (workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net.key/.crt).
Obsidian section tracks shared settings but ignores workspace.json, workspace-mobile.json, cache, graph.json.
Local throwaway test creds ignored: .cd-drive.sh/.env, .cd-history.sh (with globbed variants).
the-one-system scratch ignored as 'regenerable; not source': build-prep/the-one-system/discovery/ and fusion-scope/.
A specific stray root debug log mcp_registration_debug.log and ops/cli/telemetry.jsonl are ignored as generated runtime data.
standouts: Secrets and live TLS private keys are ignored both by glob AND by exact hostname filename (workstation001.tail777bc2.ts.net), with explicit 'live EC private key, NEVER commit' comments.
Comments encode rationale (e.g. '.data/ — written by the live system, never code') turning the ignore file into documentation.
conventions: Follows repo law that runtime store data lives separate from code and is never committed.
Heavily commented to explain WHY each group is ignored — consistent with the repo's self-describing discipline.
Mixes glob patterns with exact filenames for defense-in-depth on secrets.
concerns: An exact-hostname TLS filename hardcoded here will not protect a cert/key for a different host; relies on the glob to catch others.
notes: Git ignore file whose comments double as documentation of which artifacts are runtime/secret/regenerable and why none of them belong in git.
questions: Is the build-prep/the-one-system/discovery and fusion-scope scratch truly regenerable from committed inputs?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (config); the explanatory comments match the patterns they annotate.
intention_signals: Keep the repo as pure source: exclude all secrets, live TLS material, per-user Obsidian state, venvs, and machine-regenerable runtime/scratch, while documenting the reasoning inline.
novelty: The inline rationale comments and exact-hostname TLS key entries make the ignore file self-describing about the repo's secret and runtime boundaries.
contribution: Contributes the concrete exclusion boundary between committed source and runtime/secret/scratch artifacts, with annotated rationale.
summary_for_embedding: A heavily-commented .gitignore for the company repo excluding venvs, runtime store data, secrets, live TLS keys, Obsidian state, and regenerable the-one-system scratch.
intention_for_embedding: To keep secrets, live credentials, runtime data, and regenerable scratch out of git while documenting why each is excluded.
